topic: TCP congestion control algorithms compared — a side-by-side analysis of Cubic, BBR, and Vegas on a simulated lossy WAN link, with throughput and fairness charts under controlled conditions.
outline_hint: TCP Congestion Control: Cubic vs BBR vs Vegas
Why congestion control still matters in the datacenter-age WAN
Cubic: loss-based, additive-increase multiplicative-decrease
BBR: model-based, estimating bandwidth and RTT
Vegas: delay-based, sensing queue buildup
Test setup: Mahimahi link emulator, 50 Mbps / 60 ms / 1% loss
Results table: throughput, retransmit rate, fairness index
Fairness concern: BBR vs Cubic competing on shared bottleneck
When to choose which: practical guidance by scenario

Projection engines: Kafka Streams for real-time, Flink for batch
